message:
fix(db): quote "exclusive" as SQL reserved keyword and harden timestamp parsing

The column name "exclusive" in the file_reservations table is a SQL
reserved keyword. When using FrankenConnection (DbConn) instead of the
C-backed SQLite binding, unquoted reserved keywords cause parse failures.
This went unnoticed with the C binding because it was more permissive.

SQL keyword quoting (across 5 files):
- cli/lib.rs: quote fr.exclusive -> fr."exclusive" in all 8 SELECT and
  INSERT queries for file reservation list/create/conflict-check paths
- cli/robot.rs: quote in 4 queries (status, reservations, analytics
  conflict detection, navigate)
- db/queries.rs: quote in FILE_RESERVATION_SELECT_COLUMNS_SQL constant
  and in create_file_reservations conflict-check query
- tui_screens/explorer.rs: quote in reservation pressure card query

ACTIVE_RESERVATION_PREDICATE fix (db/queries.rs):
- Replace GLOB negated character class pattern (NOT GLOB '*[^0-9.+-]*')
  with nested REPLACE() chain that strips all valid numeric characters
  and checks for empty result. FrankenConnection's GLOB implementation
  does not support negated character classes in the same way as C SQLite,
  so this equivalent approach works across both backends.

Schema PRAGMA fix (db/schema.rs):
- Quote DELETE in PRAGMA journal_mode = 'DELETE' to prevent parse
  ambiguity with the DELETE statement keyword

TUI poller improvements (tui_poller.rs):
- Add parse_raw_ts() and parse_text_timestamp() functions for robust
  timestamp parsing from heterogeneous SQLite column types (integer,
  real, text as microseconds, text as YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.ffffff)
- Add days_from_civil() helper using Howard Hinnant's algorithm for
  chrono-free date-to-epoch conversion
- Remove debug eprintln!() statements from fetch_reservation_snapshots

Also updates conformance test database binary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
